Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
description: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: Why Congress picked VD Satheesan as Kerala CM: Allies, cadre mood, optics
VD Satheesan named next Kerala chief minister as Congress ends suspense
description: After days of intense consultations, the Congress has chosen VD Satheesan as the next chief minister of Kerala. The announcement follows the Congress-led UDF's decisive victory in the state elections, ending the Left Democratic Front's second consecutive term. Satheesan emerged as the preferred candidate over KC Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala.

description: Former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee appeared before the Calcutta High Court, urging protection for citizens amidst allegations of post-poll violence. She accused police of inaction and highlighted incidents of attacks, intimidation, and threats, seeking urgent judicial intervention to ensure people's safety.

'Question paper circulated through couriers': Jaipur's Biwal family at centre of NEET paper leak
description: The CBI has arrested five and detained four in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, uncovering a multi-state distribution network originating in Pune. A Rajasthan family, the Biwals, is central to the probe, with investigators reconstructing the leak's path through couriers and digital channels. The investigation was triggered by a whistleblower's alert.

Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s combined net worth in 2026
description: Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s combined net worth in 2026 is estimated at around $35 million to $40 million. Most of that wealth comes from Sinner’s tennis prize money, ATP success, and endorsement deals with global brands. Hasanovic contributes through modeling, fashion partnerships, and social media promotions. Sinner’s growing success in international tennis continues driving the couple’s financial growth.

title: ‘They hijacked the body’: RG Kar parents move court, demand arrest of ex-MLA Nirmal Ghosh
description: The mother of a rape-murder victim, now an MLA, has moved court seeking the arrest of three Trinamool Congress leaders, alleging they attempted to destroy evidence and hastily cremated her daughter's body. The court has fixed June 5 for the next hearing, with the CBI stating they will submit a response regarding the arrests.

title: Gurgaon student held for NEET paper leak is on school banner of top achievers
description: A studious farmer's son, Yash Yadav, hailed as an achiever by his local school, has been arrested by Rajasthan police in connection with the NEET-UG paper leak.  Investigators suspect he received and sold a 'guess paper' for lakhs, potentially implicating him in a wider racket that has disrupted the medical entrance exam for lakhs of aspirants.

title: West Bengal makes ‘Vande Mataram’ must in school morning assemblies
description: West Bengal schools must now sing 'Vande Mataram' daily during morning assembly. This directive follows a Union Home Ministry notice. School heads face confusion regarding the mandatory singing of the state song 'Banglar mati Banglar jal' alongside 'Vande Mataram' and the National Anthem. Some schools have already begun implementing the order, while others await clarification before the summer break concludes.

title: Speeding Audi rams scooter in Bengaluru, kills man; wife, daughter hospitalised
Zverev denied entry at Rome restaurant before Italian Open defeat
description: Alexander Zverev endured a difficult 24 hours in Rome after being denied entry to a famous restaurant and then crashing out of the Italian Open. The World No. 3 was recently turned away from the popular Japanese restaurant Zuma in central Rome because he did not have a reservation. Reports also claimed that the restaurant staff failed to recognise the German tennis star when he arrived with his brother Mischa Zverev and a friend for dinner.
